title: -Contract No. 32300096 Amendment: SCS Engineers for Landfill Post-Closure Maintenance and Monitoring Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32300096 with SCS Engineers for maintenance, monitoring, and compliance
services related to the closed landfill at Cesar Chavez Park, increasing the contract
amount by $1,834,000 for a total not to exceed amount of $4,078,022.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for this amendment in the amount of $1,834,000 is available in the FY 2026
adopted budget in the Zero Waste Fund (601).
sections.current_situation:
The City relies on the resources and expertise of qualified consultants to maintain the
landfill gas collection and flare system at Cesar Chavez Park, and to monitor surface
and subsurface conditions at the closed landfill in compliance with regulatory agencies.
The current contract with SCS Engineers (“SCS”) for landfill post-closure maintenance,
monitoring, and compliance services was authorized by Council on September 20,
2022, by Resolution No. 70,532-N.S. An amendment to the current contract was
authorized by Council on May 14, 2024 by Resolution No. 71,330-N.S. This contract
and second amendment cover services for the period of January 1, 2023 to June 30,
2026 in an amount not to exceed $2,244,022.
From January 1, 2023 to May 30, 2025, payment for services has been authorized to
SCS for approximately 94% of the total approved contract amount. The expedited
contract spending was triggered by a series of Notices of Violations (“NOVs”) issued by
the Bay Area Air Quality Management District (BAAQMD) Board to the City,
preparations for a variance request to BAAQMD for these violations, and a subsequent
hearing with the BAAQMD Hearing Board. Following the hearing, BAAQMD denied the
City’s variance request and issued an Abatement Order. The order mandates
compliance actions and conditions beyond routine monitoring and maintenance
activities that City staff can accomplish.

sections.background:
Cesar Chavez Park (previously known as North Waterfront Park) is located at the
Berkeley Marina on the site of the former ninety-acre Berkeley Landfill. The Park has
been open to the public for recreational use since 1991. BAAQMD issued the City an
operations permit to monitor and maintain the landfill gas collection system,
groundwater wells, leachate wells, and other surface and subsurface conditions in
accordance with state regulations. Landfill post-closure compliance services are
required for a forty to fifty-year period before the site can be permanently closed.
Beginning on November 22, 2021, the BAAQMD issued a series of NOVs related to
non-compliant operation of the landfill gas collection system. On February 6, 2024,
BAAQMD voted to issue an Abatement Order which requires the City to take a series of
compliance actions to bring the closed landfill to full compliance. Actions include
locating missing gas collection wells, testing, repairs of the gas collection system; and
the development and implementation of a process to identify any gas seeping offsite.
Page 2
Page 3 of 5
Contract No. 32300096 Amendment: CONSENT CALENDAR
SCS Engineers for Landfill Post-Closure
Maintenance and Monitoring Services July 29, 2025
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Hiring SCS to continue providing landfill post-closure engineering services will help
ensure that greenhouse gases that emanating from the closed landfill are captured and
incinerated before entering the atmosphere.
sections.rationale:
The services requested are required to comply with the landfill post-closure operations
permit and the Abatement Order. Continuing with the same contractor ensures ongoing
compliance measures are uninterrupted and consistent. The City lacks the in-house
expertise or resources needed to fulfill these obligations.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative action was considered since compliance with federal, state, and regional
regulatory requirements is mandatory.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Public Works, (510) 981-6396
Ronald A. Nevels, Manager of Engineering, Public Works, (510) 981-6439

1: Resolution – Amendment to Contract with SCS Engineers

Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32300096 AMENDMENT: SCS ENGINEERS
WHEREAS, the City is required to maintain the landfill gas collection and flare system at
Cesar Chavez Park, as well as monitor surface and subsurface conditions at the closed
landfill, in accordance with a post-closure permit issued by the Bay Area Air Quality
Management District (“Air District”); and
WHEREAS, the City does not have the resources or expertise to fulfill the obligations of
the landfill post-closure permit and requires the services of qualified firms to fulfill these
obligations; and
WHEREAS, on June 28, 2022, the City released a Request for Qualifications
(Specification No. 22-11521-C) seeking firms to provide professional services for the
landfill post-closure maintenance and monitoring project at Cesar Chavez Park and
SCS Engineers was determined to be the best qualified bidder; and
WHEREAS, on September 20, 2022, by Resolution No. 70,532-N.S., the City Council
authorized Contract No. 32300096 with SCS Engineers for landfill post-closure
maintenance and monitoring, in an amount not to exceed $714,022 for the contract
period of January 1, 2023 to June 30, 2026; and
WHEREAS, on May 14, 2024, by Resolution No. 71,330-N.S., the City Council
amended Contract No. 32300096 with SCS Engineers for landfill post-closure
maintenance and monitoring, increasing it by an amount of $1,530,000 for a total
amount not to exceed $2,244,022; and
WHEREAS, an Abatement Order was issued for the closed landfill by the Air District on
February 16, 2024, which mandates compliance actions and conditions beyond routine
monitoring and maintenance activities; and various orders have been issued by the San
Francisco Bay Regional Water Quality Control Board (RWQCB) requiring testing,
reporting, and other work beyond routine monitoring, and these actions these actions
are estimated to require $1,834,000 of additional fundin
